---
name: commit
description: Split the current git worktree into one or more meaningful commits with clean staging boundaries and commit hygiene. Use when the user asks to commit current changes, wants related changes grouped into multiple commits, or needs help preparing commit messages without disturbing unrelated local modifications.
---

# Commit

Use this skill to turn the current worktree into reviewable git commits.

## Workflow

1. Inspect `git status --short`, the relevant diffs, and repository instructions before staging anything.
2. Partition the worktree by concern.
   - Keep unrelated user changes unstaged and untouched.
   - Prefer the smallest coherent commit that still preserves behavior.
3. Stage only the files or hunks that belong together.
4. Review `git diff --cached --stat` and `git diff --cached` before each commit.
5. Write a meaningful commit message from the staged diff instead of using generic phrasing.
6. Commit with any repository-required flags or trailers.
7. Re-check `git status --short` after each commit and repeat until the requested work is committed.

## Rules

- Never revert, discard, or absorb unrelated local changes just to make the worktree look clean.
- If staged and unstaged hunks coexist in one file, verify that the split still leaves both states coherent.
- Run the narrowest useful validation before committing when a fast, directly relevant check exists.
- If the user did not provide commit-message text, derive it from the staged content and the repository's message conventions.
- If a required precondition is missing, say so instead of forcing a partial or misleading commit.

## Output

- Report the commits created, in order.
- Note any remaining unstaged or uncommitted changes.
- Call out skipped validation or blockers explicitly.

---
name: commit
description: Split the current git worktree into one or more meaningful commits with clean staging boundaries and commit hygiene. Use when the user asks to commit current changes, wants related changes grouped into multiple commits, or needs help preparing commit messages without disturbing unrelated local modifications.
---

# Commit

Use this skill to turn the current worktree into reviewable git commits.

## Workflow

1. Inspect `git status --short`, the relevant diffs, and repository instructions before staging anything.
2. Partition the worktree by concern.
   - Keep unrelated user changes unstaged and untouched.
   - Prefer the smallest coherent commit that still preserves behavior.
3. Stage only the files or hunks that belong together.
4. Review `git diff --cached --stat` and `git diff --cached` before each commit.
5. Write a meaningful commit message from the staged diff instead of using generic phrasing.
6. Commit with any repository-required flags or trailers.
7. Re-check `git status --short` after each commit and repeat until the requested work is committed.

## Rules

- Never revert, discard, or absorb unrelated local changes just to make the worktree look clean.
- If staged and unstaged hunks coexist in one file, verify that the split still leaves both states coherent.
- Run the narrowest useful validation before committing when a fast, directly relevant check exists.
- If the user did not provide commit-message text, derive it from the staged content and the repository's message conventions.
- If a required precondition is missing, say so instead of forcing a partial or misleading commit.

## Output

- Report the commits created, in order.
- Note any remaining unstaged or uncommitted changes.
- Call out skipped validation or blockers explicitly.

I might also consider closing any team panes I already have open, as that seems like a good idea to keep things tidy. Finally, I’ll use shell commands to do the necessary checks and actions efficiently.

payload.message:
I found the net integrated changes are already cleanly separable into **two task contexts**:

1. **worker-1 / formatter-import-context**
   - `codegen/generator.go`

2. **worker-2 / template + regression test**
   - `codegen/languages/go/templates/type.go.tmpl`
   - `codegen/generator_test.go`

The remaining `omx(team): auto-checkpoint ...` and merge commits are just noisy transport history, so I’m rewriting the local branch into two Lore-style commits on top of `origin/perf`, preserving the exact final tree.

payload.input:
// @exec: {"yield_time_ms": 10000, "max_output_tokens": 14000}
const cwd = "/Users/zchee/go/src/github.com/zchee/spanner-manager";
const cmd = `
set -euo pipefail

backup_branch="backup/organize-team-auto-checkpoints-$(date +%Y%m%dT%H%M%S)"
git branch "$backup_branch"

base_ref="origin/perf"

git reset --soft "$base_ref"
git restore --staged .

git add codegen/generator.go
cat > /tmp/spm-commit-1.txt <<'EOF'
Keep generated Go import resolution stable outside the generator cwd

The generator was formatting source with an empty filename, so
golang.org/x/tools/imports had no stable file context and could
resolve imports relative to the caller environment. This commit
anchors formatting to the generated file path so the generated
package is resolved consistently regardless of the caller cwd.

Constraint: First pass must stay generator-local and preserve the current generated API shape
Rejected: Broader generator cleanup | unnecessary to close the cwd-sensitive formatter bug
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep imports.Process tied to the generated file context unless a broader generator contract change is intentionally planned
Tested: go test ./codegen -run 'TestGenerator_Generate_(TemplatePathOverride_LegacyFullFileTemplates|CustomTypeImports|CompileGeneratedOutput)$' -count=1
Tested: go vet ./codegen
Not-tested: go test ./codegen -count=1 still includes broader stale expectation failures outside this formatter-only lane

Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>
EOF
git commit -F /tmp/spm-commit-1.txt

git add codegen/languages/go/templates/type.go.tmpl codegen/generator_test.go
cat > /tmp/spm-commit-2.txt <<'EOF'
Prevent generated column switch helpers from dropping fields

The Go type template was ranging a nonexistent top-level .Fields
value even though the generator passes fields through .Type.
This commit makes the switch helpers iterate .Type.Fields and
adds regression coverage that proves columnsToPtrs and
columnsToValues render representative field cases.

Constraint: Lane scope is limited to the Go type template and generator regression tests
Rejected: Broader template-contract hardening | beyond the requested task-context cleanup
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep switch-helper tests aligned with generated output semantics instead of stale literal expectations
Tested: go test ./codegen -run 'TestGenerator_Generate_(BasicFiles|ColumnSwitches)$' -count=1
Tested: go vet ./codegen
Tested: git diff --check
Not-tested: go test ./codegen -count=1 still fails in broader expectation mismatches at CompositePrimaryKeyOrder, UUIDImportsShareThirdPartyGroup, and WritableColumns

Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>
EOF
git commit -F /tmp/spm-commit-2.txt

printf -- '--- FINAL STATUS ---\\n'
git status --short --branch
printf '\\n--- NEW LOG ---\\n'
git log --oneline --decorate -8
printf '\\n--- BACKUP BRANCH ---\\n%s\\n' "$backup_branch"
printf '\\n--- FINAL DIFF VS origin/perf ---\\n'
git diff --stat "$base_ref"..HEAD -- codegen/generator.go codegen/languages/go/templates/type.go.tmpl codegen/generator_test.go
`;
const res = await tools.shell_command({
  command: cmd,
  workdir: cwd,
  timeout_ms: 120000
});
text(typeof res === "string" ? res : JSON.stringify(res));
